major_issues: M1.1: FID comparability — Sound2Scene comparison in same table as within-protocol results (Section 4.1, Table 2)
M1.2: Architecture novelty overclaim — dual-path gating mechanism adapted from Uni-ControlNet without clear attribution (Section 3.3.3, Section 1.3)
M1.3: Arm E (CLAP vs. ImageBind) results entirely absent from all tables despite being defined as an ablation arm (Section 3.4)
M1.4: Per-city training/validation/test image counts not reported; data summary table missing (Section 3.2.1)
M2.1: Per-cell median LPIPS confounded by reference image quality, density bias, and camera artifacts — decomposition analysis absent (Section 3.5.1)
M2.2: Cross-city acoustic data asymmetry — London uses BN-LOUD + Noise-Planet, NYC and Singapore use Noise-Planet only; no sensitivity analysis (Section 3.2.2)
M2.3: MGWR bootstrap bandwidth CI computation method not specified; non-overlapping CI claim is the paper's key spatial argument but method is unreproducible (Section 3.5.4, Table 4)
M2.4: Spatial error model (SEM) reported only as R² footnote; AICc, residual Moran's I, block CV RMSE omitted from Table 3 (Section 4.3)
M3.1: ISO 12913 pleasantness and eventfulness are model-predicted approximations, not measurements; MLP prediction accuracy on ARAUS and SoundingEarth not reported (Section 3.2.2, 3.3.1)
M3.2: Noise-Planet temporal and behavioral sampling bias not characterized; time-of-day quasi-global MGWR bandwidth may reflect data bias (Section 3.2.2, Section 5.3)
M3.3: No perceptual validation of generated image acoustic coherence; FID/LPIPS do not confirm ISO 12913 semantic coupling in outputs (Section 5.1)
M3.4: ISO 12913-2 (2018) and ISO 12913-3 (2019) not cited despite direct relevance to measurement methodology (Section 2.3)
path_to_acceptance: 1. Report Arm E (CLAP vs. ImageBind) results in Table 2 with full metrics and significance tests, or report pilot results in supplementary material with exact values
2. Validate ISO 12913 MLP prediction head: report held-out ARAUS Pearson r and RMSE for pleasantness and eventfulness; calibration check on SoundingEarth; reframe abstract accordingly
3. Specify MGWR bootstrap bandwidth CI computation method explicitly and reproducibly; confirm full MGWR re-runs on 2,000 resamples
4. Remove Sound2Scene FID from Table 2 or place it in a separate clearly labeled comparative reference table with explicit benchmark-incomparability caveat
5. Clarify that dual-path gating mechanism is adapted from Uni-ControlNet (Zhao et al., 2023); remove any implied claim of a new fusion operator
6. Add SEM as a full row in Table 3 with adjusted R-squared, AICc, residual Moran's I, and block CV RMSE per city
7. Add supplementary sensitivity analysis: London MGWR with Noise-Planet-only LAeq vs. BN-LOUD + Noise-Planet LAeq
8. Characterize Noise-Planet time-of-day measurement distribution per city; discuss implications for time-of-day quasi-global MGWR bandwidth

9. Add pilot human evaluation of generated images for ISO 12913 pleasantness correspondence, or flag explicitly as a limitation that distributional metrics do not confirm acoustic-visual semantic coherence
10. Cite ISO 12913-2 (2018) and ISO 12913-3 (2019) in Section 2.3 and methodology
11. Add data summary table with per-city training, validation, and held-out test image counts before and after temporal exclusions
12. Resolve Figure 4 numbering conflict (generation grid vs. LPIPS choropleth maps are both labeled Figure 4)
notes: All three reviewers converge on Major Revision. The spatial methodology is unusually rigorous for a GeoAI paper and the core novelty is strong. The six major categories of issues are all addressable without new data collection or new training runs, with the possible exception of M3.3 (perceptual validation study). If perceptual validation is infeasible, a clearly scoped limitation statement is an acceptable alternative. Recommend the authors prioritize M1.3, M2.3, M3.1, and M3.3 as the four most substantive methodological gaps.
